description: Azure-specific rules — apply ONLY to nodes where cloud_provider is AZURE

rules.title: Azure VM Has Open Port With Public Exposure
rules.message: [NODE_ID] is an Azure VM with OPEN_PORT flag and PUBLIC exposure — AZ-VM-001 violated. No Network Security Group restriction exists. Direct internet access to VM ports. Source: Azure Security Benchmark v3 NS-1.
rules.fix: Add Network Security Group rules to {node_id}. Allow only required ports (e.g. 443 for HTTPS, 22 only from specific IPs). Remove or restrict all open port rules. Apply NSG at both subnet and NIC level.
judge_scenarios: Is my Azure VM secure?
Which Azure resources have open ports?
Does my Azure setup follow Security Benchmark?
What are my Azure network security issues?
Which Azure VMs are internet-facing?

rules.title: Idle Azure VM Must Be Deallocated Not Just Stopped
rules.message: [NODE_ID] is an Azure VM with utilization below 20% costing over $100/month — AZ-VM-002 violated. NOTE: In Azure, simply stopping a VM still charges for compute. You must DEALLOCATE it to stop billing. Source: Azure Cost Management Best Practices.
rules.fix: DEALLOCATE {node_id} from Azure Portal or CLI: az vm deallocate --name {node_id}. Do NOT just stop it — stopped Azure VMs still incur compute charges. Deallocated state stops all billing except storage.
judge_scenarios: How do I stop Azure billing for idle VMs?
Which Azure resources am I wasting money on?
What is the difference between stopped and deallocated in Azure?
How much can I save on Azure?
Which Azure VMs should I deallocate?

rules.title: Azure Blob Storage Must Not Be Publicly Accessible
rules.message: [NODE_ID] is an Azure Blob Storage container with PUBLIC exposure and PUBLIC_BUCKET flag — AZ-STORAGE-001 violated. Anonymous public read access is enabled. Anyone on the internet can read this data. Source: Azure Storage Security Guide.
rules.fix: Disable anonymous public access on {node_id}. In Azure Portal: Storage Account → Configuration → Allow Blob public access → Disabled. Also set container access level to Private. Enable Azure Defender for Storage.
judge_scenarios: Is my Azure storage secure?
Can anyone access my Azure Blob data?
Which Azure storage containers are public?
What are my data exposure risks in Azure?
Does my Azure storage follow security best practices?

rules.title: High-Risk AWS Node Has Cross-Cloud Dependency on Azure
rules.message: [NODE_ID] is an Azure resource that a HIGH RISK AWS node DEPENDS ON — AZ-CROSS-001 violated. If the AWS node is compromised, the attacker can follow the DEPENDS_ON edge to reach this Azure resource. Blast radius spans across clouds. Source: Multi-cloud security blast radius containment principle.
rules.fix: Isolate {node_id} from the high-risk AWS dependency. Options: 1) Fix the AWS node risk first (close ports, fix IAM). 2) Add authentication between AWS node and Azure resource. 3) Use Azure Private Link to restrict access.
judge_scenarios: Is my Azure data safe if AWS is compromised?
What is the cross-cloud blast radius?
Which Azure resources are at risk from AWS issues?
How does an AWS breach affect my Azure resources?
What are my cross-cloud security risks?
